text:
Joely Myrlene Sullivan, Angel wings Monday, December 2018.
visitation will held from 1-2:00 p.m., Friday, December 2018 Schooler Funeral Home Brentwood Chapel, 4100 Georgia Memorial services will follow 2:00 p.m., funeral home.
Arrangements Schooler Funeral Home.
Joely youngest daughter Delores "Dee" Myrle Davis.
proudest moments being chosen Miss Sanford Fritch High School.
After high school, Joely followed passion became Licensed Barber.
enjoyed making people happy with good haircut.
Without doubt, proudest accomplishment life giving birth three beautiful children, Daxton, Quinton Hannah.
They were sunshine world.
married Rusty Sullivan moved Louisiana starting chapter life.
However, never Texas heart even Hostile territory, remained avid Dallas Cowboys fan.
preceded death mother, Davis; sisters, Lisa Davis Christi Callen.
Left cherish memory include father, Myrle Davis; devoted husband, Rusty Sullivan; sons, Daxton Quinton Stokes; butterfly garden, daughter Hannah Stokes; stepdaughter, Miranda Saunders; special niece, Ashley Callen; special nephew, Ryne Callen wife, Mollie; treasured niece, Chelsey Hess; nephew, Chris Stiffler; cherished brother law, David Callen; wonderful gifts, grandchildren Lainey Penelope Stokes; beloved sister law, Robin Murphy husband their sons, Sean, Connor Brendan; very special aunt, Shirley Sullivan; father mother law, Harold Barbara Sullivan loved dearly.
family requests lieu flowers, donations made your local food bank, humane society, charity your choice.

Artstor shares several collections that document important religious sites architecture throughout Asia.
These include: Mellon International Dunhuang Archive (Buddhist cave shrines Dunhuang, China); Shuilu'an Temple (Northwestern sculpture Shuilu'an Temple Lantian, China); Deepanjana Danda Klein Arno Klein: Cave Temples Ellora, India (Buddhist, Hindu, Jain cave shrines Ellora, India); Beyond Taj: Architectural Traditions Landscape Experience South Asia (Cornell University Library); Getty Research Institute: Carl Jennifer Strom: Korean Buddhist Monasteries.
